title: Wave-1: e2e/support/db-seed.ts — userId-scoped + new domain seeders
acceptance_criteria: NEW file (do NOT modify the existing perf-db-seed.ts — coexist by domain). Prisma client against DEV_DATABASE_URL.

seedCompletedScan({userId?}): a completed multi-domain Scan + Findings spanning Security/Performance/Accessibility/SEO (+ optional P5 compliance findings) with grades, for the report-page state matrix; supports userId (owned) and userId:null (anonymous public report).
seedUserWithScans({userId,count}): N Scan rows owned by userId for dashboard pagination/empty/populated states.
seedDomainVerification({userId,domain}): a DomainVerification row for verify/active-test flows.
seedScanLifecycle({userId,state}): state ∈ {RUNNING (with ScanEvent progress rows), TIMEOUT (partial findings persisted, status TIMEOUT), COMPLETED}. Pure seeded rows — NEVER calls /api/v1/scans.

Every seeder returns a cleanup() that deletes exactly the rows it created (best-effort; globalSetup truncate is the real isolation guarantee). userId-scoped rows use the provided userId so user-scoped page queries (where userId = ...) return them; anonymous variant only for the public report page.
npm run typecheck passes; imports cleanly under Playwright esbuild + @/* alias; no overlap of exported names with perf-db-seed.ts.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of the userId-scoped vs anonymous seeding distinction
